title: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
summary: The investigation is said to center on whether Ms. Carroll committed perjury in civil lawsuits against Donald J. Trump, whom she accused of sexual assault.

title: White House Dismisses Outline of ‘Unofficial’ Iran Deal
summary: Plus, hunting fish with a bow and arrow. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Cornyn’s Defeat in Texas Runoff Underscores Trump's Grip on the G.O.P.
summary: Senator John Cornyn lost to his MAGA-aligned challenger, Ken Paxton, by 28 percentage points. It was a historically poor showing.

title: Cornyn’s Defeat Fuels Tensions With President Trump in Senate G.O.P.
summary: Senators are angry President Trump turned on a respected former leader whom they consider a loyal Republican. Now Mr. Trump faces resistance from his own embittered ranks.

title: Meeting Voters, a Republican Tries to Outrun His Party’s Woes
summary: Representative Mike Flood of Nebraska is still meeting with constituents long after most G.O.P. members of Congress have concluded it is too politically dangerous to do so.

title: Alabama Republicans Ask Supreme Court to Allow New Congressional Map in Redistricting Fight
summary: Republican leaders in the state have asked the justices to clear the way for a congressional map that a lower court found discriminated against Black voters.

title: Trump Says He Feels No Political Pressure to Make an Iran Deal
summary: President Trump held out hope for a peace agreement, but said high oil prices would not force his hand.

title: Trump Threatens Oman Over the Strait, Then Says, ‘They’ll Be Fine.’
summary: President Trump said there would be serious repercussions if Iran and Oman move forward with a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz.

title: To Make a Deal With Trump, Iran Wants Billions of Its Money Unfrozen
summary: The money has become a sticking point in talks, with Iran insisting that meaningful negotiations cannot begin without the funds’ release.

title: Jill Biden’s Reaction to Biden’s 2024 Debate: ‘He’s Having a Stroke’
summary: “I had never, ever seen Joe like that,” the former first lady told CBS News. “Before or since.”

title: Biden Sues Justice Dept. to Block Release of Tapes
summary: The former president argued that the Justice Department has a responsibility to protect the privacy of conversations he had with a former ghostwriter.

title: Inventor of the Basque Cheesecake Plans to Retire. His Secret: He Prefers Chocolate
summary: Santiago Rivera is widely credited with creating the “burnt” cheesecake in the 1980s, though he doesn’t love the spinoffs it has spawned. Decades later, he’s preparing to hand over his kitchen to his children.

title: Reflecting Pool Contract Has ‘Inflated’ Profit Margin, Government Analysis Finds
summary: The firm that was given a no-bid contract to fix the troubled landmark is charging 20 percent. The typical profit margin is 6 percent to 12 percent, internal records show.

title: National Park Entrance Fees Are Funding Trump’s D.C. Projects
summary: The administration is spending at least $67 million worth of fees paid by visitors to national parks on fixing D.C. fountains and the Reflecting Pool.

title: Former Judges Urge Inquiry Into Deal Trump Struck With I.R.S.
summary: The motion was particularly significant because it asked the judge overseeing the initial suit against the I.R.S. to examine the terms of the deal.

title: Court Orders Customs Chief to Address Compliance on Refunding Tariffs
summary: The surprising demand to appear at a hearing suggested new concern about the Trump administration’s efforts to repay the full $166 billion owed from illegally imposed tariffs.

title: California Tries to Block Trump Involvement in Election Procedures
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation that tries to stop outside officials from taking ballots or getting involved in ballot processing.

title: Rubio’s India Visit Yields No Major Deals to Repair U.S.-India Relations
summary: The secretary of state visited India to reassure the South Asian giant that it can still rely on the United States. India did not gain much from the visit.

title: F.B.I. Arrests C.I.A. Official With $40 Million in Gold Bars in His Home
summary: The only charge lodged against David Rush is that he inflated his academic credentials and obtained military leave pay worth tens of thousands of dollars.

title: Matthew Perry’s Assistant, Kenneth Iwamasa, Sentenced to More Than 3 Years in Prison
summary: Kenneth Iwamasa injected Mr. Perry with the ketamine that killed him. He is the last of five defendants to be sentenced in the case.

title: In Utah, Measles Sickens Babies and Others Who Can’t Be Vaccinated
summary: Many of those who can’t be vaccinated, including pregnant women and immunocompromised people, are also at high risk of serious complications.

title: Why the Ebola and Hantavirus Outbreaks Have Confounded Scientists
summary: The types of Ebola and hantavirus worrying officials are very different from the species identified decades ago, raising new questions about how to respond.

title: Uganda Closes Border With Congo as Ebola Fears Rise
summary: Seven confirmed cases of the virus have already been reported in Kampala, the capital, but officials say the country has robust disease surveillance.

title: ’60 Minutes’ Journalist Sharyn Alfonsi Loses Deal After Dispute With Bari Weiss
summary: Sharyn Alfonsi, whose segment on a brutal Salvadoran prison was pulled abruptly in December, said that CBS News and its top editor, Bari Weiss, had let her contract expire.

title: The $400 Million Showdown Between a Billionaire and a California Mayor
summary: The owner of the Los Angeles Rams and the City of Inglewood are in a dispute over Hollywood Park and SoFi Stadium, which is about to host World Cup matches.

title: Thornton Wilder’s Last Play Vanished Into Thin Air. Or Did It?
summary: Decades after “The Emporium” failed to open on Broadway in 1954, one man went on a quest to find it.

title: Ferrari’s First Electric Car Runs Into Backlash in Italy and Beyond
summary: Memes mocked the new model, analysts questioned its appeal and investors sold the automaker’s stock. A former Ferrari chairman warned of “the destruction of a legend.”

title: Knicks Fans Confront High Ticket Prices for NBA Finals Games at MSG
summary: New York Knicks fans, still celebrating the team making it to the N.B.A. finals, are confronting high ticket prices for the games at Madison Square Garden.

title: Tiler Peck’s New Ballet Expands the Idea of What Female and Male Dancers Can Be
summary: The idea of sisterhood and brotherhood flows through “Symphonie Espagnole,” Peck’s new work for New York City Ballet. We dissect two sections.

title: What Five Years in Big Law at WilmerHale Taught Me
summary: Despite all our good intentions, the legal system — that thing we were charged to protect — kept getting worse.

title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
summary: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.

title: In Flint, Cash for Pregnant Women Leads to Better Outcomes for Babies
summary: New research suggests money with no strings attached can promote better health, but other studies have seen mixed results.

title: Ball State Settles With Ex-Employee for $225,000 Over Charlie Kirk Posts
summary: Ball State University is the latest institution to agree to pay workers who lost their jobs over their posts about the conservative activist.

title: Man Charged With Hate-Crime Killing of Gay Dancer Claims Self-Defense
summary: Dmitriy Popov was 17 when he stabbed O’Shae Sibley, a 28-year-old dancer, at a gas station. Mr. Popov has been charged with murder as a hate crime.

title: Condé Nast Pays Over $400,000 to 3 Journalists Fired Over Protest
summary: The former workers were among a group of employees who confronted the company’s head of human resources about layoffs last fall.
